# The Cumulus Network

## Cooperative Efficiency, Freely Chosen

**The Cumulus Network operates two sister corporations to contribute to solving
our current unsustainability crisis: one for profit, the other not.**

1. Our LLC, [_SubtleTea Solutions (STS)_](https://subtleteasolutions.com), is a
   computational and organizational science services company dedicated to
   realizing lucrative yet sustainable business models.
2. Our nonprofit, the [_Generation of Participation in Democracy
   (GPD)_](https://gpdamericas.org), is a California Public Benefit Corporation
in compliance with California Department of Justice registration. We are
awaiting the GPD's tax-exempt status 501(c)(3) determination, **EIN: 41-4992916**.
    - Its first _Flagship Organization_ is the [_Instituto
      Dourados_](https://institutodourados.org). The _Instituto's_ mission is to
      provide computational epidemiological tools for public health planning for
      increasing Indegenous autonomy, _Grande Dourados_ region in Mato
      Grosso do Sul, Brazil.


## Board of Directors

Our directors serve identical roles at both GPD and STS. This is temporary
scaffolding as we continue to identify and fill key roles at GPD and STS.
The current board will remain as Network leadership for the foreseeable future,
but specific roles within organizations will be adjusted as necessary for
success.

One of the key goals for Q3 and Q4 of 2026 is to establish a policy for a
directional "air gap" so that money from the non-profit (GPD) never flows to the
for-profit LLC. This will particularly benefit from consolidated leadership
until it is finished and agreed upon.


### Matthew A Turner, PhD <span class=role>President</span>

![](images/MatthewTurner.png){.member-photo}

Dr. Matt Turner is the Founder and CEO of the GPD and STS. His mission is to
translate and disseminate his and his colleagues' innovative research to have
the real-world impact it should.

Dr. Turner spends his days writing his book, creating
scientific hardware and software, writing and publishing websites, and organizing and executing all business details across
both organizations, including acting as responsible party for all tax and
compliance issues.

[Dr. Turner's website](https://mat.phd)

### Izabela Mauricio de Rezende, PhD <span class=role>Secretary</span>

![](images/IzabelaRezende.png){.member-photo}

Dr. Iza Rezende is Founder and Director of Instituto Dourados, the inaugural
Flagship Institute of the GPD, in addition to serving as Secretary.

Dr. Rezende will ensure that we deliver on our promise to serve the Indigenous peoples
of Dourados, and public health policy improvement in general. <br>

[Dr. Rezende on LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/izabela-rezende/)

### Joshua Rueckheim, MBA <span class=role>Treasurer</span>

![](images/JoshuaRueckheim.png){.member-photo}

Mr. Joshua Rueckheim oversees book keeping, develops controls and
ensures enforcement, and assists with structuring and executing
forecasts and other business processes. In short, Mr. Rueckheim
applies his decades of expertise in business management to ensure the integrity
of the Cumulus Network's organizations.


Mr. Rueckheim is currently Finance Director for the Philadelphia Hospitality
Investment Levy and for [Alta Management
Services](https://www.altamsi.com/our-team/). Before joining Alta, Mr.
Rueckheim worked for Mariott International for 10 years in various finance and
accounting roles.

[Mr. Rueckheim on LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/joshua-rueckheim-96241226/)


## Strategic Organization and Communication

We focus on developing an approach to social change that strategically
balances economic cooperation and competition strategically, guided by a new
integrative approach to social science.

We are staunchly anti-ideology. We will never seek to force conformity. Our
system is founded on the belief that all people should be free to choose for
themselves based on their own self-interest. Education therefore is essential so
that one can choose consciously what they _want_ in the first place, which
requires knowing how to tell good from bad---not trivial in a world of
accelerating complexity.

<!-- link to book when ready -->

In democracies, we often think social change has to be achieved through
elections, petitioning government, open opposition in the form of costly signals
of protest, and pursuing other forms of struggle as rights are increasingly
infringed upon. Certainly that has its place. But within the Cumulus Network we
focus our efforts developing the good as an antidote against the bad, instead of
fighting the bad directly.

<br>

---

<br>


{{< include _footer.qmd >}}
